title: Houses for rent in Berea, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Berea, OH
metaDescription: Searching for houses for rent in Berea, OH? Get rent benchmarks, Baldwin Wallace-area transit notes, and Section 8 facts, then contact us about nearby homes.
answerBox: Berea is a southwest Cuyahoga County suburb best known as home to Baldwin Wallace University. It's a market-rate rental area without its own published rent data, so renters typically compare it to citywide Cleveland benchmarks. Contact us to ask about voucher-friendly homes we manage in nearby Cleveland neighborhoods.

body: Berea is a Cuyahoga County suburb southwest of downtown Cleveland, most widely known as the home of Baldwin Wallace University. The area mixes owner-occupied single-family blocks near the campus with rental houses and duplexes scattered throughout its residential streets.

Berea's downtown historic district, centered around Front Street and Bagley Road, sits within walking distance of the university and includes small storefronts, restaurants, and Coe Lake Park. Renters who work in Berea but want a shorter commute to downtown Cleveland or the airport sometimes widen their search to neighboring suburbs and Cleveland's west side, where rental houses tend to be more numerous.

Our managed portfolio is currently concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods, with a smaller presence on the west side — we don't have confirmed inventory inside Berea today. If you want a house in or near Berea, [tell us what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) and we'll follow up, or check our full list of [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) for what's currently available. Renters comparing options a few miles northwest may also want to look at [houses for rent in Brook Park](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-brook-park-ohio/).

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent a house near Berea?
body: Berea doesn't have its own published rent index, so renters generally use the wider Cleveland-area market as a reference point. Zumper's July 4, 2026 report placed the area's median asking rent at $1,250 a month, up 4.2% year-over-year, while RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report — which weights toward larger professionally-managed communities — found a higher average of $1,564 for 787 square feet. Expect an individual Berea house's rent to depend heavily on its size, age, and proximity to Baldwin Wallace's campus.

Berea sits along the I-71 corridor southwest of Cleveland, giving quick highway access toward downtown and toward Cleveland Hopkins International Airport a few miles to the northeast. GCRTA, the region's public transit agency, operates bus routes connecting Cuyahoga County suburbs like Berea to the wider Cleveland transit network, though Berea isn't served directly by RTA's rail lines.

items: **Highway:** I-71 corridor, southwest of downtown Cleveland
**Airport:** A few miles from Cleveland Hopkins International Airport
**Transit:** GCRTA bus routes; no direct rail line into Berea

sections.h2: Section 8 and housing vouchers in Berea
body: As in the rest of Ohio, there's no state law requiring a landlord to accept Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8), and Berea is not currently one of the Cuyahoga County suburbs — Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale — that have passed their own source-of-income protection ordinance as of July 2026. Ask directly about a listing's voucher policy before applying. CMHA runs the county's Housing Choice Voucher program, including for renters looking in Berea, and its [preliminary application](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) stays open year-round with placement by rolling lottery. Every home we manage accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready — read more in our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/).

Electric service in Berea generally comes from The Illuminating Company (FirstEnergy) rather than Cleveland Public Power, which is limited mostly to addresses inside Cleveland's city limits. Natural gas is typically provided by Enbridge Gas Ohio (formerly Dominion East Ohio), though some Cuyahoga County suburbs use Columbia Gas of Ohio instead — confirm which one serves your address. Cleveland Water, one of the country's largest municipal water systems, supplies many suburbs in addition to the city itself.

sections.h2: Climate and moving considerations near Berea
body: Like most of Cleveland's west side, Berea sits outside the region's heaviest lake-effect snow belt, which concentrates in the higher-elevation areas south and east of Lake Erie. The Cleveland area overall averages roughly 60–70 inches of snow a season, with lake-effect activity heaviest from November through January, but west-side and southwest suburbs like Berea typically see less accumulation than communities on the city's higher-elevation East Side. Renters moving in winter should still plan for regular snow removal and confirm in the lease whether snow and lawn service are included.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in Berea?
faq.a: Some landlords accept vouchers, but Ohio law doesn't require it and Berea hasn't passed its own source-of-income protection ordinance, so acceptance varies. CMHA administers the county's Housing Choice Voucher program and can help you search. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready — contact us to ask about homes we manage nearby.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house near Berea?
faq.a: Berea doesn't publish its own rent index, so the closest benchmark is the wider Cleveland market. Zumper's July 2026 report puts the area median around $1,250 a month, while RentCafe's professionally-managed-apartment average runs higher, near $1,564. An individual Berea house's rent depends on its size, age, and location relative to Baldwin Wallace University.

faq.q: What is Berea, Ohio known for?
faq.a: Berea is a Cuyahoga County suburb southwest of downtown Cleveland, best known as the home of Baldwin Wallace University. Its small downtown historic district around Front Street and Bagley Road, plus Coe Lake Park, sits within walking distance of campus. Berea is along the I-71 corridor, a few miles from Cleveland Hopkins International Airport.

faq.q: Is Berea served by RTA rail?
faq.a: No. Berea has no direct RTA rail station; GCRTA bus routes connect it to the wider transit network, and the nearest rail access is the Red Line station at Cleveland Hopkins International Airport, a few miles to the northeast, which links directly to downtown Cleveland's Tower City.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage homes in Berea?
faq.a: Not currently — our confirmed inventory is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side, with a smaller west side presence. Contact us and tell us you're looking near Berea, and we'll follow up about voucher-friendly homes we manage nearby that may fit your commute.

faq.q: How do renters get from Berea to downtown Cleveland?
faq.a: Most renters drive via I-71, which runs along Berea's edge and connects directly to downtown Cleveland. There's no RTA rail station in Berea itself; GCRTA buses cover the suburb, and the nearest rail access is the Red Line station at Cleveland Hopkins International Airport, a few miles away, for a car-free trip into Tower City.
